title: antirrhinum majus subsp. glutinosum
intro: Antirrhinum majus subsp. glutinosum, also known as sticky snapdragon, is a subspecies of the Antirrhinum majus plant, which is a flowering perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Antirrhinum majus subsp. glutinosum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
